summary:
Maternal screening for antiphospholipid antibodies, specifically anti-Ro/SSA and anti-La/SSA, is indicated because they are the primary causative agents behind congenital heart block via neonatal lupus. These antibodies cross the placenta and lead to autoimmune-mediated damage in the fetal myocardium, necessitating routine testing during pregnancy.  

(Note: Final answer box not required.) 

The final summary must follow exactly two sentences only — no more than that.

Okay, let me create an accurate yet concise version based strictly upon Agent2’s actual reasoning:

When a胎儿 echo reveals congenital heart block, the mother should be screened for antiphospholipid antibodies, particularly anti-Ro/SSA and anti-La/SSA, since these autoantibodies crossing the placenta induce neonatal lupus leading to conductive abnormalities. This condition represents up to 95% of prenatal diagnoses of congenital atrioventricular block, establishing clear pathophysiological linkage between maternal immune status and fetal outcome.
